title: Chern-Simons Gauge Variation Is a Boundary Term
summary: For abelian U(1) Chern-Simons theory, a gauge transformation A -> A + dLambda changes the action by a boundary integral, which is the anomaly-inflow term on manifolds with boundary.
assumptions: The bulk gauge field is abelian.
Boundary contributions are retained instead of discarded.
regime: Abelian 2+1-dimensional Chern-Simons response on a manifold that may have boundary.
scope: Minimal calculation behind the anomaly-inflow argument for chiral edge modes.

math_expressions.context: Gauge variation reduces to a boundary term.

symbols.meaning: gauge parameter in A -> A + d Lambda

failure_modes: On a closed manifold the same calculation gives zero, so no anomaly inflow follows.
retrieval_hints: Use when the user asks where the boundary anomaly literally comes from in the Chern-Simons variation.
